content.text: How can I correctly execute a specific callback method when processing a node during a tree traversal?

This function serves as an event hook for the tree structure. It first retrieves any registered callbacks associated with the provided node using `self.get_callbacks(node)`. If a callable method is found, it executes that method by passing the current node.

```python
def leave(self, node):
        """walk on the tree from <node>, getting callbacks from handler"""
        method = self.get_callbacks(node)[1]
        if method is not None:
            method(node)
```

The code successfully invokes the necessary callback logic, ensuring customized actions run when a node is processed. Be aware that the function only triggers one specific handler if multiple exist.
What happens if multiple methods are registered for this node? Will it execute all of them?

Based on the implementation details in your code, calling `self.get_callbacks(node)[1]` means the function will only ever attempt to run the single method stored at index 1 and ignore any other handlers.
